name: mMIG Hybrid Flow
description: MIG baseline + mMIG optimization stages in blif2mig_2

mode.description: Primary optimization objective. depth minimizes logic levels; area minimizes gate count.

mig_flow.description: Majority baseline schedule used before/with mMIG stages.

mig_allow_zero_gain.description: Allow zero-gain majority rewrites. May increase exploration and runtime.

rw_override.description: Enable explicit rewrite parameter overrides (rw_cut, rw_cut_limit) for the majority phase.

rw_cut.description: Cut size for rewriting when rw_override is enabled.

rw_cut_limit.description: Cut enumeration limit for rewriting when rw_override is enabled.

mmig_flow.description: mMIG schedule preset. round_robin is the default balanced sequence.

mmig_max_iters.description: Maximum rewrite iterations per mMIG rewrite stage.

mmig_stage.description: Where to run mMIG stages relative to majority flow: pre, post, or both.

mmig_cec.description: Enable equivalence checking guards for mMIG stages. Recommended for correctness.

mmig_allow_zero_gain.description: Allow zero-gain mMIG rewrites. Can increase search but may add runtime.

mmig_enable_sr5.description: Enable SR5 rule path (disabled by default for stability).

mmig_advanced.description: Enable advanced post-native mMIG stages (resub/cut/refactor stack).

mmig_advanced_rounds.description: Number of advanced optimization rounds when mmig_advanced is enabled.

mmig_adv_no_exact.description: Disable advanced exact-rewriting stage to reduce regressions/rollbacks.

mmig_adv_no_balance.description: Disable advanced balancing stage to avoid depth-oriented area regressions.

mmig_dont_cares.description: P2: Enable don't-care-aware resubstitution/refactoring/exact-rewriting for circuits under 10K gates.

mmig_interleaved_seeding.description: P3: Re-seed minority candidates between advanced optimization rounds.

mmig_dual_inv.description: P1: Enable dual-inversion propagation (2-of-3 inverted fanins) with fanout-aware gain.

mmig_auto_sr5.description: P7: Automatically enable SR5 algebraic rewriting rule when CEC is active.
